item.title: Security Blogs
item.description: I have compiled a list of security blogs below. For a period of about 3 weeks, I had tried to keep up with these blogs through an RSS feed reader. It was a futile effort and not something I would recommend to the average security person. I ended up spending a majority of my free time reading about criminals doing crime when I would have much rather been learning something new or making something cool.

item.title: Threat Intelligence Notes
item.description: These are my notes on threat intelligence. Intelligence Intelligence is high fidelity information that has been analyzed and can be used to influence decision making. Cyber Threat Intelligence (Cyber (Threat (Intelligence))) Threat Intelligence is intelligence pertaining to adversaries. Cyber Threat Intelligence is Threat Intelligence pertaining to the cyber domain. CTI involves understanding adversary capabilities, motivations, and goals. CTI finds usefulness in its application to intrusion detection and incident response.

item.title: Goals for February 2020
item.description: Reviewing January 2019 Action Items read Malware Data Science - Joshua Saxe with Hillary Sanders was on pace to finish, but got sidetracked with a project inspired by it finish A Guide to Japanese Grammar - Tae Kim did not work on this at all =( continue Remembering The Kanji 1 - James W. Heisig to reach 2000 kanji did not keep up with SRS while on vacation and had to relearn a bunch ended up at 1600 read Count Zero - William Gibson good book 50x situps per day + gym PPL program 6 days per week did this; started at end of December my apartment’s gym sucks, but it’s usable not fun going to the gym in January; people don’t know how to clean up after themselves try hiking in the frigid Winter once and probably never go outside again went once on a nice day figure out what dark matter is i’m more confused now February 2020 Action Items find a place to move to or take another vacation at end of the month job hunt finish Malware Data Science - Joshua Saxe with Hillary Sanders finish A Guide to Japanese Grammar - Tae Kim continue Remembering The Kanji 1 - James W.

item.title: Goals for January 2020
item.description: Reviewing December 2019 Action Items nothing! I’m on vacation, WOOO! hot wine and Mozart January 2020 Action Items read Malware Data Science - Joshua Saxe with Hillary Sanders finish A Guide to Japanese Grammar - Tae Kim finish Remembering The Kanji 1 - James W. Heisig to reach 2200 kanji read Count Zero - William Gibson 50x situps per day + gym PPL program 6 days per week try hiking in the frigid Winter once and probably never go outside again figure out what dark matter is

item.title: Goals for December 2019
item.description: Reviewing November 2019 Action Items finish Practical Malware Analysis - Michael Sikorski and Andrew Honig I finished the book, but did skip over some sections that I thought were obsolete I did not finish all of the exercises; some of this is bc the samples were written for XP and my FLARE VM is on 7 continue A Guide to Japanese Grammar - Tae Kim got to around halfway through the book studying Japanese is fun continue Remembering The Kanji 1 - James W.

item.title: Goals for November 2019
item.description: This is my first goal tracking post. Initially, I was tracking these in a text file. When I made this site, I moved it over to the resources section, but found that it wouldn’t scale well and that posts would be a better organization method. It would also force me to acknowledge my failures rather than editing the month’s items. Reviewing October 2019 Action Items As November is when I started tracking these, there is nothing to review.

item.title: Goals Methodology
item.description: Not to seem like I’m having a midlife crisis at 26 or anything, but I feel like I’ve wasted a lot of time doing much of nothing important. There is so much that I would like to learn that I sometimes feel overwhelmed. The following details how I plan to work on climbing that mountain. Method I recently started using a system for personal improvement that I found worked for me:

item.title: About
item.description: This Site Hello there! Welcome to my brain’s dumping ground. The inspiration to create a personal website came from reading through the monthly discussions on Hacker News about the advantages of doing so. Also, I like reading security blogs and would like to post my own research findings here while practicing my technical writing. That said, I want to use this site, not as a professional portfolio, but more as a means to organize and share my knowledge, ideas, thoughts, and resources.

item.title: Books
item.description: This is my reading list. As you will probably be able to tell, I’m a shill for no starch press. It’s probably the cute art on the covers that got me hooked. Current/Upcoming Reads Practical Malware Analysis - Michael Sikorski and Andrew Honig A Guide to Japanese Grammar - Tae Kim Remembering The Kanji 1 - James W. Heisig Remembering The Kanji 3 - James W. Heisig Neuromancer - William Gibson Count Zero - William Gibson Mona Lisa Overdrive - William Gibson Malware Data Science - Joshua Saxe with Hillary Sanders Backlog Security The Hardware Hacker - Andrew “bunnie” Huang Rootkits and Bootkits - Alex Matrosov, Eugene Rodionov, and Sergey Bratus Practical Binary Analysis - Dennis Andriesse Real-World Bug Hunting - Peter Yaworski Black Hat Python - Justin Seitz Attacking Network Protocols - James Forshaw The Hacker Playbook - Peter Kim Serious Cryptography - Jean-Philippe Aumasson Game Hacking - Nick Cano PoC||GTFO - Manul Laphroaig Novels Snow Crash - Neal Stephenson Pattern Recognition - William Gibson Recommendations Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?

item.title: Data Science
item.description: Courses A Clear Roadmap for ML/DL - guide on reddit Andrew Ng’s ML Course - the best?, but need to google for python versions of code deeplearning.ai’s DL Courses - tensorflow eww Books Python Data Science Handbook - Jake VanderPlas Hands-On Machine Learning With Scikit-Learn & TensorFlow - Aurélien Géron Book of R - Tilman M. Davies Data Visualization with JavaScript - Stephen A.
